surface_verification.justification: Client-only DragOverlay prop change (dropAnimation={null}). Ran client/e2e/flows/board-dnd-status-decouple.spec.ts (3 tests) against an isolated single-process stack (built SPA via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR + tsx, temp HOME, :3948) to confirm the drag still functionally relocates + reopens with no regression — 3/3 passed in real Chromium. The animation smoothness itself is requires-manual-visual-judgment (user UAT on rebuild).

behaviors.behavior: The board's <DragOverlay> is configured with dropAnimation=null (no return-to-origin drop animation)
behaviors.evidence: client TaskBoardColumns.dropanim.test.tsx 'disables the return-to-origin drop animation' PASSED (captures the prop dnd-kit's DragOverlay receives)

behaviors.behavior: Dragging a card across columns still functionally relocates + reopens (no regression from the prop change)
behaviors.evidence: E2E board-dnd-status-decouple.spec.ts 3/3 PASSED in real Chromium (:3948)

behaviors.behavior: The on-drop animation looks smooth (no visible flip-back to origin)
behaviors.reason: the perceived smoothness of the drop transition on a real GPU/compositor is a visual judgment headless Chromium cannot make; confirmed by user UAT after rebuild
